post.description:
Initiate the outbound process for a stock item

**barcode**:
Barcode of the product to be removed

Returns the location for the item to be removed (compliant with FIFO)
This can be followed by a post call to /confirm

Confirm the outbound process for a stock item

**rack_location**:
Location of the item to be removed

Returns a message indicating success

403.description: You are not authorized to confirm this outbound process

post.description:
Cancel the outbound process for a stock item

**rack_location**:
Location of the item to be removed

Returns a message indicating success

OAuth2 compatible token login, get an access token for future requests.

- If 2FA is enabled for the user, returns a temporary token with `is_2fa_required=True`.
- If 2FA is disabled, returns a full access token.

Initiate 2FA setup for the current user.

Generates a new TOTP secret and returns:
- **secret**: The raw secret key.
- **qr_code_url**: The provisioning URI.
- **qr_code_image**: Base64 encoded PNG image of the QR code.

Verify the code to ENABLE 2FA.

Requires the user to provide a valid code generated by their authenticator app
to confirm the setup and activate 2FA on their account.

Complete login with 2FA code.

Requires:
- **token**: The temporary access token received from `/login` (when `is_2fa_required=True`).
- **code**: The TOTP code from the authenticator app.

Returns a full access token if successful.

401.description: Invalid token or User not found

post.description:
Change the current user's password.

Requires:
- **old_password**: The user's current password for verification.
- **new_password**: The new password (minimum 8 characters).
- **confirm_password**: Confirmation of the new password (must match).

Returns a success message if the password is changed successfully.

Register a new user request.

This endpoint handles the initial registration of a user. The user is created with
`is_active=False` and requires administrator approval.

409.description: User with this login already exists

post.description:
Create a default administrator account.

This is a utility endpoint to seed an admin user if one does not already exist
based on the `ADMIN_LOGIN` setting.

schema.title: Response Create Admin Api V1 Users Create Admin Post

put.description:
Approve a pending user registration.

Activates a user account (`is_active=True`) that was previously created via registration.
Only accessible by administrators.

Reject a pending user registration.

Permanently deletes a user account that is pending approval.
Only accessible by administrators.

get.description:
Get current user details.

Retrieves the information of the currently authenticated user.

schema.title: Response Get All Warehouse Workers Api V1 Users Warehouse Workers Get
Response Get All Warehouse Workers Api V1 Users Requests Get

post.description:
Create a new rack.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

Import racks configuration from a CSV file.

The CSV file should contain rack definitions. The import process will:
- Validate the CSV format and content.
- Check for conflicts with existing stock items (e.g., if new rack definition is smaller than existing items).
- Create new racks or update existing ones.

The import process is executed asynchronously using Celery.
The result of the import process can be checked using the GET method on the /import/{celery_task_id} endpoint.

get.description:
Get the result of the import process.

Example use would be fetching every second to check status of the import process.
The task_id is returned after requesting an import with the POST method on the /import endpoint.

put.description:
Update an existing rack.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

400.description: Rack with this designation already exists

delete.description:
Delete a rack.

Can only be executed by an admin user.
Will fail if the rack is not empty (contains stock items).

get.description:
Get rack details by ID.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

post.description:
Create a new product definition.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

schema.title: Response Get Product Definitions Api V1 Product Definitions  Get

post.description:
Upload an image for a product definition.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

Bulk upload images for product definitions.
Matches files to products by filename (stored in photo_path).
Processing is done in background.

get.description: Get the status of the bulk upload task.
Get a specific product definition by ID.

delete.description:
Delete a product definition and its associated image.

Can only be executed by an admin user.

post.description:
Import product definitions from a CSV file (Asynchronous).

Can only be executed by an admin user.

get.description: Get the status/result of the import task.

post.description:
Trigger generation of a report.
Returns a task_id to poll for status.

**report_type**:
- **expiry**: Expiry date report.
- **audit**: Full warehouse audit report.
- **temp**: Temperature report (TO-DO).

Optional filters (only for expiry and temp report):
- **rack_id**: Filter by specific rack.
- **barcode**: Filter by product barcode.

get.description: Check the status of a report task.

schema.title: Response List Reports Api V1 Reports  Get

post.description:
Recognize a product from an uploaded image.

This endpoint accepts an image file, saves it securely, and queues a background task
to perform object recognition using the configured AI model.

Returns a task ID which can be polled for status and results.

Submit feedback image for a specific product.

This endpoint allows authenticated users to upload correct images for a product ID.
These images are stored and used for future model retraining to improve accuracy.

get.description:
Trigger the AI model retraining process.

This endpoint initiates a background Celery task to retrain the AI model using
all accumulated feedback and training data. This is an admin-only operation.

post.description:
Reset the AI model.

This endpoint deletes cached model files and resets the service. Use this when switching hardware (CPU/GPU) to force a fresh model download.

Bulk upload training data for a product.

Allows administrators to upload multiple training images for a specific product ID
at once. These images are added to the dataset for retraining. This is an admin-only operation.

get.description:
Check the status of a background task.

Retrieves the current state (PENDING, STARTED, SUCCESS, FAILURE) and result
of a specific Celery task (e.g., retraining or prediction) given its ID.
